channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: A Historical Look at The American Legion Weekly and Its 1925 Covers
item.description: First published on July 4, 1919, The American Legion Weekly served as the forerunner to today’s American Legion Magazine. It provided an essential connection for veterans returning from World War I, o...

item.title: Closing Arguments in OpenAI Trial Highlight Disputes Between Musk and Altman Over Company Conduct
item.description: The trial examining whether OpenAI and its executives misused the nonprofit for personal gain is nearing its conclusion in Oakland, California. Elon Musk's lawyer, Steven Molo, questioned the credibil...

item.title: Albanese Government declares neo-Nazi group a prohibited hate organisation
item.description: The Albanese government has officially listed the neo-Nazi group previously known as the National Socialist Network (NSN) as a prohibited hate organisation. This decision follows legislation enacted a...

item.title: President Samia dissolves PSSSF Board of Trustees after 14 months in office
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has officially dissolved the Board of Trustees of the Public Service Social Security Fund (PSSSF), just 14 months after its formation. This decision was announced in a st...

item.title: Strengthening Critical Thinking and Civic Education in Philippine Schools
item.description: The article argues that the decline in Philippine education, particularly in liberal arts and humanities, has contributed to a populace vulnerable to manipulation by politicians and bad actors. It hig...

item.title: Montreal Takes Command with 6-3 Win Over Buffalo in Game 5
item.description: In a thrilling Game 5 of the NHL playoffs, the Montreal Canadiens overcame the Buffalo Sabres with a 6-3 victory, taking a 3-2 series lead. Early in the game, Buffalo seemed to dominate, scoring three...

item.title: Post Office to contest second CAPTURE scandal case in Court of Appeal
item.description: The Post Office has announced its intention to oppose a second legal case related to the CAPTURE accounting scandal at the Court of Appeal. This follows a previous series of cases in which numerous su...

item.title: Arsenal’s Potential Earnings and Benefits from Winning the Premier League
item.description: As the 2025/26 Premier League season nears its end, Arsenal fans are buzzing with hope that the club can finally end its long wait for a league title. Winning the Premier League would not only bring p...

item.title: Political Analysts Clash Over U.S.-China Relations During CNN Debate
item.description: During a Thursday evening segment on CNN's 'NewsNight,' a heated debate erupted between political analysts John Avlon and Joe Borelli over U.S.-China relations. The discussion escalated after Borelli,...

item.title: Christopher Walken's Early Life and Career as a Child Actor in the 1950s
item.description: Before becoming an iconic actor known for his unique delivery and eccentric roles, Christopher Walken, originally known as Ronnie Walken, had a prolific career as a child actor in the 1950s. Born in 1...

item.title: UK Government Trials AI to Reduce Costs of Court Transcriptions for Victims
item.description: For years, victims of crime in the UK have faced significant barriers when trying to access court records, largely due to prohibitive costs and bureaucratic processes. The Ministry of Justice (MoJ) ha...

item.title: Key Legal Issues in Elon Musk’s Lawsuit Against OpenAI Co-Founders
item.description: Nine jurors in California are deliberating on Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI cofounders, including Sam Altman, and Microsoft. The case centers on whether Musk's donations to OpenAI were misused, a...

item.title: Street Photography Captures San Francisco Women's Fashion in the Early 1970s
item.description: The early 1970s in San Francisco were a time of rich cultural and fashion experimentation, with streets becoming a canvas for personal expression. Women’s fashion reflected a blend of bohemian influen...

item.title: Seven golfers, including Scottie Scheffler, share lead after first round of PGA Championship
item.description: After the opening round of the PGA Championship at Aronimink Golf Club, Scottie Scheffler emerged as one of seven players tied for the lead, shooting a 3-under 67. Scheffler managed two long birdie pu...

item.title: Trump and Xi Conclude Key Trade Talks During Beijing Summit
item.description: United States President Donald Trump met Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Friday to finalise several high-level trade agreements and discuss global strategic issues. Trump described Xi as a ...

item.title: Australia and the UK Reach Eurovision Final: Highlights from Thursday's Semi-Final
item.description: Australia has secured a place in the Eurovision Song Contest final following a standout performance by Delta Goodrem, who delivered her power ballad 'Eclipse' with notable flair. The achievement has e...

item.title: Kagame Calls for African Unity to Protect the Continent's Resources
item.description: Rwanda’s President Paul Kagame has raised alarm about the ongoing exploitation of Africa by powerful foreign nations. Speaking at the Africa CEO Forum in Kigali, he urged African countries to stop bei...

item.title: Five dead in firecracker factory blast in Dewas, Madhya Pradesh; owner arrested
item.description: In Dewas district of Madhya Pradesh, a tragic explosion at a firecracker factory in Tonk Kalan area on May 14, 2026, has claimed the lives of five workers, while 23 others are receiving treatment for ...

item.title: Biographer raises concerns over Trump's late-night social media activity
item.description: President Donald Trump’s frequent late-night posts on Truth Social have raised concerns from mental health experts, according to biographer Michael Wolff. Since returning to office in January, Trump h...

item.title: Egerton University Clarifies Political Neutrality After Brief Facebook Post Supporting Matiang’i
item.description: Egerton University found itself in the spotlight after a Facebook post endorsing Fred Matiang’i for the 2027 presidential race briefly appeared on its official page. The post, which included 15 photos...
